name: LoqApplication
label: LOQ application
description: Applies the Limit of Quantification (LOQ) to water chemistry CSV files. Values below the LOQ for a given element or compound are replaced by LOQ / 2, following the standard half-LOQ substitution method for left-censored analytical data. Each LOQ parameter represents the minimum concentration reliably measurable by the ICP-Forest laboratory equipment for that element. Every substitution is recorded in a traceability log.

parameters.name: param_WeightedConductivity
parameters.label: LOQ Weighted Conductivity (µS/cm)
parameters.description: Limit of quantification for weighted conductivity, in µS/cm.

parameters.name: param_NH4N
parameters.label: LOQ NH4-N (mg/L)
parameters.description: Limit of quantification for ammonium nitrogen (NH4-N), in mg/L.

inputs.name: input-data
inputs.label: Transformed data ZIP
inputs.description: ZIP archive containing tab-separated CSV files with water chemistry data, one per SiteCode per subprogram. Each CSV must include a header row with column names matching those expected for LOQ application (e.g. 'NH4N(mg/l)', 'CA(mg/l)'). Produced by the DataTransformation wrapper, but compatible with any CSV set sharing the same column naming convention.

outputs.name: output-data
outputs.label: LOQ-corrected data ZIP
outputs.description: ZIP archive with the same CSV files after LOQ substitution. Values below the LOQ have been replaced by LOQ / 2.

outputs.name: output-log
outputs.label: Substitution log
outputs.description: Tab-separated log file listing every substitution made: filename, row number, column name, original value, LOQ threshold and replacement value.

author: LifeWatch ERIC
